subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Tangent Lines & Linearization
stimulus: The graph of the function $f$ shown above consists of six line segments. Let $g$ be the function given by $g(x) = \int_{-4}^{x} f(t)\, dt$.

parts.prompt: Does $g$ have a relative minimum, a relative maximum, or neither at $x = 1$? Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: Does $g$ have a relative minimum, a relative maximum, or neither at $x = 1$? Justify your answer.

parts.prompt: Suppose that $f$ is defined for all real numbers $x$ and is periodic with a period of length 5. The graph above shows two periods of $f$. Given that $g(5) = 2$, find $g(10)$ and write an equation for the line tangent to the graph of $g$ at $x = 108$.
rubric_criteria: Given that $g(5) = 2$, find $g(10)$ and write an equation for the line tangent to the graph of $g$ at $x = 108$.
